title: M1 backtest run-safety rules - prevent killing the wrong (productive) run
registered_by: user directive: 'Sicherheitsmechanismen klaeren um erneute Verwechselung auszuschliessen'

incident.what_happened: Two concurrent M1 backtests ran (203857Z + 211142Z). The productive one (CPU-active, in path-sim) was killed because 'productive vs hung' was judged from prediction_cache file timestamps instead of measured CPU. The actually-hung run (211142Z, 0% CPU for ~3h) was the one that should have been killed.
root_causes: DUPLICATE: two M1 backtests were allowed to run at once (no single-run guard).
INFERENCE: liveness was inferred from timestamps, not measured from CPU.

rules.rule: At most ONE M1 backtest may run at a time. Before launching, run tools/_m1_run_guard.py preflight; only launch if safe_to_launch=true (no PRODUCTIVE run exists).
NEVER kill a backtest based on file timestamps, dir age, or assumptions. Classification PRODUCTIVE vs IDLE_OR_HUNG MUST come from measured CPU growth (tools/_m1_run_guard.py inventory).

On a CONFLICT, kill ONLY the dirs listed in the guard's kill_candidates (always the non-productive ones). If verdict is CONFLICT_ALL_IDLE_INVESTIGATE or CONFLICT_MULTIPLE_PRODUCTIVE_DO_NOT_KILL -> stop and investigate, do NOT kill.
rules.enforced_by: human/agent follows guard verdict; tool never auto-kills

rules.rule: _m1_run_guard.py is read-only and never kills. Killing remains a deliberate, evidence-justified action.

When relaunching M1 reusing cached predictions (--reuse-prediction-cache or --backtest-scope path-only), use --parallel-backtest-backend thread and --n-jobs 1. The process backend with a large worker pool (32) deadlocked phase 4 silently (CPU drops to 0, no error) -> M1 never finishes. The canonical M1 path config (tests/test_r0_migration_m1.py) is thread + n-jobs 1.
rules.incident_ref: 2026-06-05: process-backend phase-4 deadlock after ~1.5h compute; recovered with thread/n-jobs 1.
rules.enforced_by: manual relaunch config; matrix path-only path already uses thread+1

verdicts.SINGLE_IDLE_INVESTIGATE: one run, but no CPU - check if hung before any action
verdicts.CONFLICT_KEEP_PRODUCTIVE: multiple runs - keep the single productive one, kill_candidates are safe to stop
verdicts.CONFLICT_ALL_IDLE_INVESTIGATE: multiple runs, none active - investigate, do not blind-kill
verdicts.CONFLICT_MULTIPLE_PRODUCTIVE_DO_NOT_KILL: multiple active runs - do not kill, escalate
